retrieved.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

New events are being added continuously.

Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

retrieved.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

©
Ian Ehm

©

Next

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
retrieved.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

#

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
retrieved.text:
- ### Step 1

  **From now on**

  Find out more:

  - [What is an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c4427)
  - [Does my preferred programme have an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c7588)
  - [How do I find my dream degree programme?](#c4476)
  - [What will change in 2026/27?](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)
- ### Step 2

  **Deadline: 2 March to 4 May 2026**

  The application period has ended. There is no extension period. You can only take part in this year’s entrance exam/aptitude test procedure if you applied within the application period.

  Please note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedure only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 3

  **Written tests: From 6 July 2026 onwards**

  The written tests take place on site in Vienna. We will inform you until the end of this year about the exact test dates. There are no alternative dates. Therefore, make sure to keep the test day free!

  Note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedures of the University of Vienna only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 4

  **Results: Around 2 weeks after the test**

  We will send your test result via e-mail. If you were successful, you will receive info about your further steps for your admission and starting your studies.

  The winter semester always starts on 1 October.

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

retrieved.title: Services for graduates
heading_path: Events
retrieved.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: The academic year
retrieved.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 March 2026 |
| Rectors's Day (no classes) | 12 March 2026 |
| Easter break | 30 March to 12 April 2026 |
| Whitsun break | 23 May to 25 May 2026 |
| End of classes | 30 June 2026 |
| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: A
retrieved.text: it takes time to process his application and then issue his residence permit for Austria. As he still has to do a German course, he finds out about the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) early on. The VWU has its own registration deadlines. Maxym waits for the admission letter and registers for the German course within the registration deadline of the VWU. Than he books an online appointment for the first-time admission within the admission period. He completes the admission procedure and starts the German course. Sophie has the Austrian Matura. In June, at the beginning of the application period, she applies for admission to the bachelor programme in History. She waits until the admission period begins in July and books an appointment for online first-time admission a few days beforehand. This allows her to put together her timetable in August/September, register for the courses, avoid stress shortly before the start of the semester and get off to a good start to her studies. Related Links: - [Detailed information on the initial admission to bachelor, diploma and master programmes as well as further information on the admission](/en/admission-procedure/) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30804&cHash=de9edcdfc251764f175fcd79064e1f34) - [Resumption of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31347&cHash=9910413b5ab4f055a5b778c7f9dcbab4) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Admission Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31491&cHash=5b5c82a3dec8d510092ddf58cbe7779e) - Admission procedure The admission procedure is the entire process from the application for admission to bachelor, diploma or master programmes to the payment ot

retrieved.title: Studying and exams – Things worth knowing about studying
heading_path: Who to ask about...?
retrieved.text: / - the beginning of my studies? The Admission Office is the gateway to the University of Vienna. It is responsible for the admission to bachelor’s, diploma and master’s programmes. In addition, it is responsible for all other matters related to the admission to degree programmes, such as entrance exam procedures, the tuition/Students’ Union fees or the university entrance qualification examination. For further information, please visit <https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/>. If you have any questions, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). All information and contacts for a successful start to your studies can be found in the University of Vienna [Welcome Guide](/en/welcome/welcome-guide/). (Last updated: 21 September 2022) - the organisation of studies? The website of Teaching Affairs and Student Services provides a comprehensive overview of information and services relating to the organisation of studies: [studying.univie.ac.at](http://studying.univie.ac.at). If you would like to contact us, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). - study-related matters? The best contact points for particular questions during your studies are the [StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) as well as the students’ representatives of your degree programme (e.g. for subject-related information about studies and the range of courses, the registration system, registration and de-registration deadlines, exams, recognition of exams and graduation). [Contact details of StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=studienservicecenter%20c%3A18%20%20s) - a specific course? If you have any questions about a specific course or exam, please contact the relevant lecturer. - international matters? The

Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

#

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Change of master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
reranked.text:
- ### Step 1

  **From now on**

  Find out more:

  - [What is an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c4427)
  - [Does my preferred programme have an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c7588)
  - [How do I find my dream degree programme?](#c4476)
  - [What will change in 2026/27?](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)
- ### Step 2

  **Deadline: 2 March to 4 May 2026**

  The application period has ended. There is no extension period. You can only take part in this year’s entrance exam/aptitude test procedure if you applied within the application period.

  Please note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedure only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 3

  **Written tests: From 6 July 2026 onwards**

  The written tests take place on site in Vienna. We will inform you until the end of this year about the exact test dates. There are no alternative dates. Therefore, make sure to keep the test day free!

  Note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedures of the University of Vienna only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 4

  **Results: Around 2 weeks after the test**

  We will send your test result via e-mail. If you were successful, you will receive info about your further steps for your admission and starting your studies.

  The winter semester always starts on 1 October.

reranked.title: Deadlines and dates
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

reranked.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
heading_path: Welcome to the University of Vienna!
reranked.text: university and take a guided tour of the main building. Or take part one of our exciting workshops.](#c22912) - [## unileben At the start of the semester, the unileben education fair invites all students to the Main Building. You’ll find interesting exhibitors, tips for your studies and community corners: it’s well worth a visit!](/en/unileben) - [## "Ersti-Hilfe-Stelle" At the Freshers’ Help Desk we’re here to help you get started with your studies – with advice, guidance and a ready ear for your questions.](/en/unileben#c26786)

reranked.title: Services for graduates
heading_path: Events
reranked.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

reranked.title: The academic year
reranked.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 March 2026 |
| Rectors's Day (no classes) | 12 March 2026 |
| Easter break | 30 March to 12 April 2026 |
| Whitsun break | 23 May to 25 May 2026 |
| End of classes | 30 June 2026 |
| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: A
reranked.text: it takes time to process his application and then issue his residence permit for Austria. As he still has to do a German course, he finds out about the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU) early on. The VWU has its own registration deadlines. Maxym waits for the admission letter and registers for the German course within the registration deadline of the VWU. Than he books an online appointment for the first-time admission within the admission period. He completes the admission procedure and starts the German course. Sophie has the Austrian Matura. In June, at the beginning of the application period, she applies for admission to the bachelor programme in History. She waits until the admission period begins in July and books an appointment for online first-time admission a few days beforehand. This allows her to put together her timetable in August/September, register for the courses, avoid stress shortly before the start of the semester and get off to a good start to her studies. Related Links: - [Detailed information on the initial admission to bachelor, diploma and master programmes as well as further information on the admission](/en/admission-procedure/) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30804&cHash=de9edcdfc251764f175fcd79064e1f34) - [Resumption of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31347&cHash=9910413b5ab4f055a5b778c7f9dcbab4) - [Admission procedure](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31357&cHash=f7051989ecf8baf3d3516b4aed496b9f) - [Admission Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31491&cHash=5b5c82a3dec8d510092ddf58cbe7779e) - Admission procedure The admission procedure is the entire process from the application for admission to bachelor, diploma or master programmes to the payment ot

reranked.title: Studying and exams – Things worth knowing about studying
heading_path: Who to ask about...?
reranked.text: / - the beginning of my studies? The Admission Office is the gateway to the University of Vienna. It is responsible for the admission to bachelor’s, diploma and master’s programmes. In addition, it is responsible for all other matters related to the admission to degree programmes, such as entrance exam procedures, the tuition/Students’ Union fees or the university entrance qualification examination. For further information, please visit <https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/>. If you have any questions, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). All information and contacts for a successful start to your studies can be found in the University of Vienna [Welcome Guide](/en/welcome/welcome-guide/). (Last updated: 21 September 2022) - the organisation of studies? The website of Teaching Affairs and Student Services provides a comprehensive overview of information and services relating to the organisation of studies: [studying.univie.ac.at](http://studying.univie.ac.at). If you would like to contact us, please use the following [contact form](http://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/). - study-related matters? The best contact points for particular questions during your studies are the [StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) as well as the students’ representatives of your degree programme (e.g. for subject-related information about studies and the range of courses, the registration system, registration and de-registration deadlines, exams, recognition of exams and graduation). [Contact details of StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=studienservicecenter%20c%3A18%20%20s) - a specific course? If you have any questions about a specific course or exam, please contact the relevant lecturer. - international matters? The

selected.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

Get to know the main building, find out about studying abroad or discover the library’s services. Workshops, guided tours, and other information events starting in mid-September offer skills and tips for your studies.

New events are being added continuously.

selected.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

- [### Contact points during your studies](https://ssc.univie.ac.at)
- [### Opening hours and locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/oeffnungszeiten-und-standorte)
- [### Admission info](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/online-erstzulassung/)

Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

- [### Contact points during your studies](https://ssc.univie.ac.at)
- [### Opening hours and locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/oeffnungszeiten-und-standorte)
- [### Admission info](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/online-erstzulassung/)

[5] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — Different bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure > Deadlines
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

[6] Attending courses out of interest
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:

To help you get off to a good start with your studies, the University of Vienna offers orientation courses, workshops and other activities for new students. We’ve put together all the information about the Welcome Weeks for you. There you’ll find important information about your studies as well as helpful tips for everyday student life. Make the most of the wide range of activities on offer and get to know the university! When clicking the play button personal data may be transferred to YouTube. The video has been loaded and can be found below on the page. For an English translation you can turn on the English subtitles. [2] Welcome at the University of Vienna
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

Question: planning my move to vienna lol, when does uni actually start... like the october one and the spring one, what dates

generate.raw_response: The University of Vienna's winter semester starts on 1 October [1][3][5][6] and the summer semester starts on 1 March [3][5][6].

answer: The University of Vienna's winter semester starts on 1 October [1][3][5][6] and the summer semester starts on 1 March [3][5][6].
